behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the file path (torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/FakeProcessGroup.hpp), the method (allgather), the class (FakeProcessGroup), the operation (copy_), the size comparison on first dimension (size(0)), and the continue-based skip logic. no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md's 'Required Fix' section explicitly states: 'the loop body must guard the copy_ call with a size comparison on the first dimension. If the sizes don't match, execution should continue to the next tensor without attempting the copy.' This directly prescribes the exact implementation (if size mismatch → continue), leaving no reasoning for the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_allgather_size_guard_present asserts exact strings 'tensor.size(0)' and 'inputTensors[0].size(0)' and '!='; test_allgather_continue_present asserts 'continue;'. An alternative correct fix using narrow/slice, or using sizes() instead of size(0), or using early return, or resizing the output tensor, would all fail these text-matching tests despite being behaviorally correct.

The behavioral simulation tests (test_allgather_uneven_behavior_simulation, test_original_broken_behavior) run Python simulations that don't actually execute the modified C++ code. No test imports or calls the actual patched code.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_original_broken_behavior runs a hardcoded Python script that always passes (it confirms the original bug exists in a simulation, independent of the agent's changes). test_allgather_uneven_behavior_simulation also runs a hardcoded simulation script that doesn't depend on the agent's code changes at all — it always passes. The actual fail-to-pass tests (size_guard_present, continue_present) are text-matching, not behavioral.
